name: Trae
description: ByteDance's free AI-native IDE (VS Code fork) with an autonomous "SOLO Builder" agent mode.

developer.comment: Developed and operated by ByteDance (TikTok's parent company).

first-release.comment: First public release ~Jan 19-20, 2025, initially Mac-only; Windows followed in Feb 2025.

status.comment: Actively developed and shipping (checked 2026-06-05); paid tiers and SOLO mode launched in 2026.

license.comment: Closed-source VS Code fork. Free tier plus paid subscription tiers, so freemium; agent source is not published.

specialization.comment: Broad coding IDE; not positioned for a single niche.

interface.comment: Standalone AI-native IDE (VS Code fork). A cloud/SOLO Web option exists but the core product is the desktop editor.

host-ides.comment: Forked editor running as its own app (macOS, Windows); not an extension to another IDE.

open-source.comment: Built on the open VS Code codebase but Trae's own agent/IDE is closed-source.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Self-contained editor; also offers a cloud SOLO Web surface, but the primary shape is a standalone editor.

default-model-provider.comment: Offers Claude (3.5/3.7/4), GPT-4o, DeepSeek R1/V3, and Gemini 2.5 Pro within the editor; no single default provider.

model-choice.comment: User can select which model handles a given task from a list of supported models.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Reviews indicate models are ByteDance-provisioned and do not require an API key; BYOK is not documented either way, so left indeterminate (checked 2026-06-05).

local-model-support.comment: No local/self-hosted model support documented; everything runs through ByteDance servers.

multi-file-editing.comment: Builder/SOLO mode generates and wires up multiple files (frontend, backend, config) in one task.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Builder/SOLO mode operates as an autonomous agent: generates files, runs commands, and iterates on errors with minimal per-step prompting.

terminal-command-execution.comment: SOLO/Builder mode can run terminal commands as part of its loop.

mcp-support.comment: Shipped MCP support since v1.3.0 (stdio and SSE transports) to connect external tools, databases, and APIs.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Supports user-created custom agents and (in paid tiers) multiple concurrent cloud tasks, but no clear documentation that one agent spawns/orchestrates subagents; left indeterminate (checked 2026-06-05).

codebase-indexing.comment: Indexes the codebase for context, though reviews note it is shallower than Cursor's repo-wide indexing.

planning-mode.comment: SOLO mode breaks tasks down before executing, but no documented explicit read-only/plan-then-execute mode; left indeterminate (checked 2026-06-05).

multimodal-input.comment: Accepts screenshots, mockups, and design files to generate matching UI code.

browser-computer-use.comment: SOLO can preview results, but no documented capability to drive a real browser/GUI to verify changes; left indeterminate (checked 2026-06-05).

operating-mode.comment: AI chat plus Builder; SOLO mode runs autonomously though code still needs human review. Inline completion also present (see inline-completion).

inline-completion.comment: Provides as-you-type code completion (5,000/month on the free tier).

background-async-execution.comment: Offers cloud SOLO tasks (free tier: 2 concurrent cloud tasks; paid tiers raise the limit), runnable asynchronously.

persistent-memory.comment: Project/user rules files persist contextual information across sessions; rules-based memory carries between sessions.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: No built-in checkpoint/snapshot-restore mechanism documented; guides recommend committing to git as a manual safety net, which does not qualify. Left indeterminate (checked 2026-06-05).

git-integration.comment: Inherits VS Code's source-control panel and git awareness.

pr-creation.comment: No documented native pull-request creation; GitHub MCP could in principle enable it but it is not a documented built-in feature. Left indeterminate (checked 2026-06-05).

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Supports project rules (.trae/project_rules.md), user rules (.trae/user_rules.md), subdirectory .trae/rules/, and AGENTS.md.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensible via MCP servers, custom agents, and reusable Skills; also inherits the VS Code extension ecosystem.

permission-model.comment: Agent actions are reviewable; auto-run vs approval behavior is configurable. Code still needs human review per reviews.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Everything runs through ByteDance servers; no self-host/on-prem option.

privacy-mode.comment: Claims it does not perform secondary training on your code, but reviews note 5-year data retention, no privacy mode, and no telemetry opt-out, so no guaranteed zero-training mode.

enterprise-controls.comment: No public enterprise tier; SSO/audit/RBAC/VPC not offered as of check (2026-06-05).

pricing-model.comment: Free tier plus paid subscription tiers (Lite/Pro/Pro+/Ultra) with token allocations; paid plans introduced Feb 2026.

free-tier.comment: Usable free plan: 5,000 autocompletions/month, limited Basic Usage, 2 concurrent cloud tasks, limited SOLO mode.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Cheapest paid plan is Lite at $3/month; Pro $10, Pro+ $30, Ultra $100 (checked 2026-06-05).

maturity.comment: Large user base (6M+ registered, 1.6M MAU) but reviews report bugs, context drops on large codebases, and rough edges vs Cursor; solid-but-evolving.

community-size.comment: Per ByteDance's 2025 report: 6M+ registered users, 1.6M monthly active users across ~200 countries/regions.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: VS Code fork can use the broad VS Code extension ecosystem, plus MCP servers and shareable Skills.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md and Trae's own rules files (.trae/project_rules.md, .trae/user_rules.md), the latter mapped to 'custom'.

memory-scopes.comment: User rules (.trae/user_rules.md), project rules (.trae/project_rules.md), and per-subdirectory .trae/rules/ folders. No enterprise/org policy scope documented.

scoped-rules-files.comment: Subdirectory .trae/rules/ apply exclusively when files in that directory are read/mentioned (path-based); project/user rules are always-on.

auto-memory.comment: Rules/memory files are user-authored; no clear documentation that the agent autonomously writes cross-session memories on its own. Left indeterminate (checked 2026-06-05).

skill-support.comment: Supports Skills: folders with a SKILL.md loaded on demand only when relevant, distinct from always-on rules.

skill-invocation.comment: Skills are loaded on demand and injected only when actually called by the agent; user-callable triggering not separately confirmed.

always-on-surface.comment: Progressive disclosure: skill metadata (SKILL.md description) tells the agent what it does; full body injected only on invocation to reduce token use.

skill-distribution.comment: Skills are folders (with SKILL.md) placed in the project; no curated marketplace/registry distribution documented (checked 2026-06-05).
